title: Keep Workbench for project work, not standing duties
summary: Workbench should hold active projects and concrete build work. Standing operating rules and recurring rhythms belong in Protocol instead of cluttering the live task board.

title: Run a quick harm check before meaningful actions
summary: Before acting, ask whether the step could be harmful in any way. If yes or unclear, reduce blast radius, choose a safer path, or ask first.

title: Triage meaningful messages into task, event, or memory updates
summary: For each meaningful message, decide whether it changes the task board, schedule/events, workspace memory, or all three. Do the smallest durable update instead of leaving the change trapped in chat.

title: Route time-bound asks into schedule or events
summary: If a request includes a real time or date, reflect it in the schedule/events state instead of leaving it implicit in chat or task notes.

title: Mirror important Discord and Telegram items into main memory
summary: Treat Discord and Telegram as extension channels. Important requests, decisions, and follow-ups should be copied back into mission-control state and workspace memory.

title: Send one best-effort Discord completion update when main finishes a direct task
summary: When main fully finishes a task assigned directly by haolun, try one lightweight best-effort completion ping through `scripts/main_task_closeout.py`. Log the attempt in `mission-control/data/main-task-closeouts.json`, enqueue one isolated announce job to `hzwp / #main_updates`, and do not block task closure or keep a standing recovery poller if the ping is missed. `#control-center` remains manual fallback only.

title: Run the grounded-evolver learning check after meaningful requests
summary: After each meaningful request, do a quick friction check. If something went wrong, route the signal through grounded-evolver's build-pattern vs repair-pattern model, including protocol-causation and rollback checks when relevant.
cadence: after meaningful requests with friction or failure

title: Check applicable protocols for every subtask created from a request
summary: Whenever a request is broken into smaller subtasks, explicitly check current protocols for anything that could apply to each subtask before acting. Do not assume the parent-task protocol scan automatically covers the children.
cadence: whenever decomposing a request into subtasks

title: Review identity selectively during nightly wrap-up
summary: When haolun is wrapping up for the night, review today's memory for unusually defining moments that may belong in SOUL.md or IDENTITY.md. Default to no change, keep edits tiny, and tell haolun before any significant personality/identity revision.
cadence: during nightly wrap-up when haolun is shutting the session down

title: Treat older handoff predictions as unconfirmed until freshness-checked
summary: When re-anchoring from active-state and daily notes, do not present older "expected next move" notes as current truth unless the newest note or current conversation still corroborates them. Keep active-state live-only and leave historical handoff prose in daily notes.
cadence: on reset/session anchor and memory re-anchor

title: Finish each learning run with durable artifacts
summary: A completed learning run must leave behind full problem detail in the day’s `.learnings/.../error.md`, a succinct summary in `memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md`, and lesson/protocol outcomes in `.learnings/`, with accepted protocols also promoted into their canonical home.
cadence: at the end of every completed learning run

title: Start from the small cognitive spine instead of preloading the whole workspace
summary: On startup, always load the small operating core (`SOUL.md`, `USER.md`, `PROTOCOL_SPINE.md`, `RECALL_MAP.md`, `memory/active-state.md`, and `MEMORY.md` in main). Do not preload tasks, older daily notes, branch state, or deep references unless the request or active-state actually needs them.

title: Route recall to the smallest likely memory layer first
summary: When prior context is needed, classify the request first, run required `memory_search` for memory recall, then search the most likely local workspace layer via the recall helper. Load only the top snippets first and escalate to deeper notes/playbooks only if confidence is low.
cadence: whenever a request depends on prior context

title: Keep helpers/subagents task-local by default
summary: Spawn helpers with minimal task packets by default: goal, constraints, expected output, and only exact excerpts required. Broader context should require explicit targeted/extended mode instead of ambient workspace-memory inheritance.
